response: The function computes the maximum of each contiguous sub‑array (window) of size `k` in `nums` by maintaining a monotonic decreasing deque of indices: it discards indices that fall out of the current window, removes from the back any indices whose values are smaller than the current element (so the deque always holds candidates in decreasing order), then the front of the deque is the index of the window’s maximum, which is appended to the result once the first full window is reached. This is the classic sliding‑window maximum algorithm using a deque to achieve O(n) time.
